Thus ends the 20th Ch., entitled Combinations for Renunciation in Kalyana Varmas Saravali.
Chapter 21.
Nabhasa Yogas
1. NABHASA YOGAS.
Yavanas have explained 1800 kinds of Nabhasa Yogas, out of which I detail below 32.
2-4. KINDS OF NABHASA YOGA.
The following are the 32 Nabhasa Yogas Nau, Chatra, Koota, Chaapa, Sringataka, Vajra, Damini,
Pasa, Veena, Kamala, Musala, Vapi, Hala, Sara, Samudra, Chakra, Maala. Sarpa, Ardha
Chandra, Yava, Kedara, Gada, Pakshi, Yupa, Yuga, Sakata, Soola, Danda, Rajju, Sakthi,
Nala and Gola. The Asraya Yogas, viz. Musala, Rajju and Nala are dealt with by Acharya
Manitha.
5-6. Gola, Yuga, Soola, Pasa, Veena (Vallaki), Kedara and Damini arc the seven Sankhya
Yogas. The two Dala Yogas viz. Sarpa (Bhujanga) and Mala are explained by Parasara
Maharishi. Savitracharya dealt with remaining 20 Yogas.
7. One born with an Asraya will obtain the good effects, viz., happiness, advantages and
